page: Amazon Web Services c8gb.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor built on the ARM64 ARMv9 architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core, backed by 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core allocation. The processor includes 36 MB of L3 cache and supports AES cryptography, though it lacks AVX, AMX, and hyper-threading capabilities. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, and network connectivity is anchored by a 33 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results demonstrate strong memory and decompression capabilities. The instance achieved a top-tier Passmark memory score and strong uncached memory bandwidth, reaching 294.53 GB/sec for read operations and 132.58 GB/sec for write operations. Single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression both registered in the top 25% tier. Geekbench testing yielded strong single-core and multi-core scores of 1894.0 and 15875.0 respectively, while database operations scored 24174.88. However, LLM inference performance on a medium 7B model was weak for prompt processing at 31.06 tokens/sec, indicating a performance tradeoff for larger machine learning workloads.

From a resource density perspective, the ARM64 architecture delivers high memory bandwidth efficiency and dedicated core execution without hyper-threading overhead. This makes the instance cost-efficient for workloads requiring predictable compute performance per resource. The server is suited for multi-threaded decompression tasks, text processing, database operations, and general compute-bound applications. It is less suited for workloads requiring local NVMe storage or heavy vector-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on the ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating at 2.8 GHz, it provides 32.0 GB of system memory, 36 MB of L3 cache, and a 33 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, with no local storage. Benchmark data highlights strong memory performance, with uncached read bandwidth reaching 294.53 GB/sec and a top-tier Passmark memory score. It also demonstrates strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and text processing capabilities. However, prompt processing for medium 7B LLM models is a notable performance tradeoff, registering as weak. This instance is suited for database operations, text processing, multi-threaded decompression, and general compute-bound workloads where dedicated core execution and high memory bandwidth are required without the need for local storage.

tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized platform delivering dedicated core execution, high memory bandwidth, and strong decompression performance for compute-bound workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory with strong uncached read and write bandwidth
33 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage bundled
Strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and text processing benchmark results
Weak prompt processing performance on medium 7B LLM models as a tradeoff
Suited for database operations, text processing, and multi-threaded decompression
